title: SEC’s Peirce says crypto vaults and onchain lending may fall under securities laws
summary: The SEC commissioner said crypto vaults, onchain lending products and other asset management tools may trigger US securities laws depending on how they are structured and operated.

title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
summary: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.

title: Vale Elects Ollie Chairman After Weeks of Governance Turmoil
summary: Vale SA shareholders elected Manuel Lino Silva de Sousa Oliveira as chairman on Wednesday, concluding weeks of boardroom turmoil sparked by an effort from one of the Brazilian mining giant’s largest investors to reshape the company’s leadership.

title: Dassault Talks With France on Jet Alternative After Airbus Flop
summary: France’s Dassault Aviation SA is in talks with French authorities to develop an alternative for the FCAS fighter jet project that was aborted after failed attempts with Airbus SE.

title: Clarity Act Latest Draft Bars Trump From Crypto Ventures—But Only Until 2029
summary: The long-awaited market-structure bill would block officials and their spouses from issuing digital assets and shield non-custodial developers, but the ethics ban expires in 2029 and enforcement rests solely with the DOJ.

title: Hugging Face CEO Thanks Chinese AI for Saving the Day After OpenAI Hack
summary: When American commercial AI refused to help investigate the breach, Hugging Face ran Chinese model GLM 5.2 locally. Its CEO now says there's an important lesson in this.

title: Benchmark sees Hut 8 becoming a ‘power-first data center REIT,’ raises target another 18%
summary: Benchmark’s new price target implies roughly 75% upside, with Hut 8 shares already up nearly 120% year to date.

title: Kraken parent Payward partners with GTN to scale xStocks internationally, beyond US equities
summary: xStocks is tokenizing equities from Hong Kong before expanding to the UK, Europe, South Korea, and other international markets.

title: The US has collected about $13bn of Venezuela’s oil money. Where is it?
summary: Washington has given diverging accounts of where the earthquake-shattered country’s funds are going

title: How a billionaire’s dream for a Florida railway went south
summary: Brightline East, the brainchild of Wes Edens, is mired in complex debt negotiations
